rulings.Case Title: DARNELL P. WITHROW VS. UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD COMPANY et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 6, 2008, line 1. PLAINTIFF DARNELL WITHROW'S Application Of Gregory T. Yaeger To Appear Pro Hace Vice For Plaintiff is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL ELAURIA et al VS. SUPERSHUTTLE INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 6, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANTS SUPERSHUTTLE INTERNATIONAL, INC AND SUPERSHUTTLE SAN FRANCISCO, INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. A reading of the complaint demonstrates that the wrongful acts of defendants were done "through former defendant SSSF". The arbitration decision in favor of SSSF and against plaintiff means that this claim is barred by strict res judicata. Vandenberg v. Superior Court (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 815, 825 fn2; Thibodeau v. Crum (1992) 4 Cal. App. 4th 749, 756-61. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAULINE EVANS VS. BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 6, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the negligence cause of action. Government Code section 815; Van Kemper v. Hayward Area Park Dist. (1979) 100 Cal. App. 3d 822, 825. Plaintiff has alleged a cause of action, the 3rd cause of action, for premises liability under Government Code section 835, a dangerous condition of public property. The demurrer to the 3rd cause of action is overruled. The demurrer to the 2nd cause of action is sustained without leave to amend without opposition from plaintiff. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCE HALL APARTMENTS VS. GARLAND GILLETTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs Against Deft[Crc 3.1700(B)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 6, 2008, line 14. PLAINTIFF PRINCE HALL APARTMENTS' Motion To Tax Costs Against Defendant [CRC 3.1700(B)] is denied. California Rule of Court 3.1700 (b)(1) codifies a 15 day deadline to file a motion to tax costs after service of the cost memo. =(302/PJM/JW)
